name: Annotated Data Models
description: Classification guides for Line of Business, Product, Document Types, and other dimensions

content.title: Annotated Data Models
content.content:
# Annotated Data Models

AI accuracy depends on having **rich text annotations** for every dimension and classification in your data model. These annotations tell the AI what each value means and when to apply it.

## The Challenge

AI models are general-purpose. They don't inherently know:
- What "Property" vs "Casualty" means in your context
- How to distinguish a "Treaty" from a "Facultative" placement
- What makes a document a "Claims Advice" vs a "Statement of Account"

## The Solution: Annotations

For every dimension value, provide:
1. **Definition** - What it means
2. **Keywords** - What to look for in documents
3. **Examples** - Typical cases
4. **Exclusions** - What it does NOT include

## Line of Business Annotations

```yaml
line_of_business:
  - code: PROP
    name: Property
    description: |
      Coverage for physical assets against damage or loss.
      Includes: buildings, equipment, inventory, infrastructure.
    keywords:
      - fire, flood, earthquake, windstorm
      - property damage, building damage
      - replacement cost, actual cash value
      - business interruption
    excludes:
      - marine cargo (see Marine)
      - aviation assets (see Aviation)
      - motor vehicles (see Motor)

  - code: CAS
    name: Casualty
    description: |
      Liability coverage for third-party claims.
      Includes: general liability, professional liability, products liability.
    keywords:
      - liability, negligence
      - bodily injury, personal injury
      - third party claims
      - defense costs, legal fees
    excludes:
      - property damage to insured's own assets (see Property)
      - workers compensation (see Workers Comp)
```

## Product Type Annotations

```yaml
product:
  - code: TREATY
    name: Treaty
    description: |
      Ongoing agreement covering a portfolio of risks.
      Automatically applies to all qualifying business.
    keywords:
      - quota share, surplus
      - excess of loss, XOL
      - aggregate cover
      - ceding commission
    identifying_features:
      - Covers multiple policies/risks
      - Has defined period (annual)
      - Automatic cession

  - code: FAC
    name: Facultative
    description: |
      Individual risk placement negotiated case-by-case.
      Each risk separately offered and accepted.
    keywords:
      - facultative, fac
      - individual risk
      - specific placement
    identifying_features:
      - Single policy/risk
      - Individual negotiation
      - Specific terms per risk
```

## Document Type Annotations

```yaml
document_type:
  - code: CLAIMS_ADVICE
    name: Claims Advice
    description: |
      Notification of a loss or claim event.
      Used to inform parties and establish reserves.
    identifying_features:
      - Contains loss date
      - References policy/contract
      - Includes reserve or estimated amount
    keywords:
      - loss advice, claim advice
      - loss notification
      - reserve, estimated loss
      - incident, occurrence

  - code: STOA
    name: Statement of Account
    description: |
      Periodic accounting reconciliation.
      Shows premiums, claims, and balances.
    identifying_features:
      - Table format with debit/credit columns
      - Period dates (quarter, month, year)
      - Running balance or totals
    keywords:
      - statement, account
      - balance, reconciliation
      - premium, claims paid
```

## How AI Uses Annotations

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Doc as Document
    participant AI as AI Agent
    participant Ann as Annotations
    participant Out as Classification

    Doc->>AI: "Property damage from flood..."
    AI->>Ann: Request LoB definitions
    Ann->>AI: Property keywords: flood, property damage
    Ann->>AI: Casualty keywords: liability, negligence
    AI->>AI: Match "flood" + "property damage"
    AI->>Out: Line of Business: Property
```

## Building Your Annotation Library

Effective AI classification requires **hundreds** of these small annotation texts:

| Dimension | Typical Values | Annotations Needed |
|-----------|----------------|-------------------|
| Line of Business | 10-20 | 10-20 |
| Product Type | 5-10 | 5-10 |
| Document Type | 15-30 | 15-30 |
| Client/Partner | 100s-1000s | 100s-1000s |
| Territory | 50-200 | 50-200 |

This is the **knowledge work** that makes AI accurate. Not model training - annotation creation.
